update_every.title: Update every
update_every.description: Data collection interval, measured in seconds. This is the effective execution resolution for checks and retries.

autodetection_retry.title: Detection retry
autodetection_retry.description: How often Netdata retries failed automatic detection jobs, in seconds. Set to 0 to disable retries.

check_name.title: Check name
check_name.description: Name that identifies this check type for chart grouping and metric naming. Charts appear under `Perfdata/<check_name>` in the dashboard. If omitted, derived from the `plugin` basename.

plugin.title: Plugin path
plugin.description: Absolute path to the Nagios-compatible check command. On Linux/macOS, must be root-owned and not writable by group or others. The command should return exit code 0, 1, 2, or 3 and may print performance data after `|`.

args.title: Arguments
args.description: Arguments passed to the check command after macro expansion.

arg_values.title: Argument macros
arg_values.description: Values exposed as `NAGIOS_ARG1` .. `NAGIOS_ARG32` for macro substitution and environment export.

environment.title: Environment
environment.description: Extra environment variables added on top of the collector's limited execution baseline. The check does not inherit the full Netdata process environment.

timeout.title: Timeout
timeout.description: Maximum time allowed for one check execution, in seconds. If the check exceeds this limit, the job state becomes `timeout`.

check_interval.title: Check interval
check_interval.description: Requested interval between regular checks, in seconds.

retry_interval.title: Retry interval
retry_interval.description: Requested interval between retry attempts while the check is in a soft non-OK state, in seconds.

max_check_attempts.title: Max check attempts
max_check_attempts.description: Maximum number of attempts before a non-OK result is treated as a hard state.

check_period.title: Check period
check_period.description: Named time period that controls when checks are allowed to run. Use `24x7` for always-on execution. Outside the allowed period, the check does not execute and the public job state becomes `paused`.

time_periods.title: Time periods
time_periods.description: Custom named schedules local to this job. `check_period` can reference any name defined here.

name.title: Name
name.description: Unique time period name referenced from `check_period`.

alias.title: Alias
alias.description: Human-readable label for this time period.

exclude.title: Exclude
exclude.description: Names of other periods to subtract from this one.

rules.title: Rules
rules.description: Allow rules for this time period.

days.title: Days
days.description: Weekdays used by `weekly` rules. If omitted, all days are allowed.

dates.title: Dates
dates.description: Explicit dates used by `date` rules, in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.

working_directory.title: Working directory
working_directory.description: Optional working directory used when running the check command.

custom_vars.title: Custom variables
custom_vars.description: Custom service variables exported as `NAGIOS__SERVICE*` environment variables.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Optional free-form notes for this job.

vnode.title: Vnode
vnode.description: Associates this data collection job with a [Virtual Node](https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/netdata-agent/configuration/organize-systems-metrics-and-alerts#virtual-nodes).

check_name.ui:help:
**Controls chart grouping and metric naming for performance data.**

Charts appear under `Synthetic > Nagios > Perfdata > <check_name>` in the dashboard.

If omitted, Netdata derives it from the `plugin` basename (e.g. `/usr/lib/nagios/plugins/check_ping` → `check_ping`, `C:\Netdata\checks\check_service.ps1` → `check_service`).

### When to set it

- When multiple jobs run the same plugin (e.g. `check_nrpe`) against different remote checks — set `check_name` to distinguish them (`check_disk`, `check_load`, etc.)

### Example metric names

With `check_name: check_memory` and a script that outputs `caches=2380912KB`:

- `nagios.perfdata.check_memory.job.execution_state`
- `nagios.perfdata.check_memory.bytes_caches`
- `nagios.perfdata.check_memory.bytes_caches_threshold_state`

plugin.ui:help:
**Absolute path to the check command.**

### Linux / macOS

The executable must meet these security requirements:
- Owned by **root**
- Not writable by group or others
- All ancestor directories owned by root and not group/other-writable
- Must be a regular, executable file
- Symlinks are resolved before validation

Example:
```
/usr/lib/nagios/plugins/check_ping
```

### Windows

Point `plugin` directly to a `.ps1`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` script. Netdata automatically invokes `.ps1` scripts through `powershell.exe` and `.bat`/`.cmd` scripts through `cmd.exe`.

Example:
```
C:\Netdata\checks\check_service.ps1
```
Path validation is not enforced on Windows — ensure scripts are stored in directories with appropriate ACLs.

### Output format

The command should return exit code `0` (OK), `1` (WARNING), `2` (CRITICAL), or `3` (UNKNOWN) and may print performance data after `|`:
```
STATUS TEXT | label=value;warn;crit;min;max
```

args.ui:help:
**Arguments passed to the check command after macro expansion.**

You can use Nagios-style macros like `$ARG1$`, `$HOSTADDRESS$`, `$_SERVICEDBHOST$` — they are expanded before execution.

On Windows, when `plugin` points to a `.ps1`/`.bat`/`.cmd` script, these arguments are appended after the script path.

timeout.ui:help: Accepts decimals for precise control (for example 1.5 for 1.5 seconds). String durations are also accepted in raw config files.

check_interval.ui:help: Requested regular check interval. Actual execution cannot be faster than `update_every`.

retry_interval.ui:help: Requested retry interval after non-OK results. Actual execution cannot be faster than `update_every`.

environment.ui:help:
**Extra environment variables for the check process.**

Checks run with a limited baseline environment (only `PATH`, `HOME`, `USER`, `TZ`, `TMPDIR`, `SHELL`, `LC_ALL` on Linux/macOS). If your script depends on additional variables, set them here.

### Example
```yaml
environment:
  ORACLE_HOME: /opt/oracle/product/19c
  LD_LIBRARY_PATH: /opt/oracle/product/19c/lib
```

custom_vars.ui:help:
**Custom service variables exposed as Nagios-style macros.**

Each entry is available as:
- Environment variable: `NAGIOS__SERVICE<UPPERCASE_KEY>`
- Macro in `args`: `$_SERVICE<KEY>$`

### Example
```yaml
custom_vars:
  DBHOST: db.example.com
  DBNAME: production
```
Use in args: `["-H", "$_SERVICEDBHOST$", "-d", "$_SERVICEDBNAME$"]`

vnode.ui:placeholder: To use this option, first create a Virtual Node and then reference its name here.
